controls.selectedNone: Click a star or planet (N / P to cycle)
controls.onscreenHint: Drag to look · click to identify · Shift-click to measure · scroll to zoom · ? for help
controls.searchAccessibleHelp: Type a name, then press up or down to highlight a match and Enter to select and track it. Escape clears the query.

screenSummary.playArea: The play area shows a first-person planetarium view of the sky for an observer on Earth, including bright stars and solar-system bodies. Sky color follows the Sun's altitude from day through twilight to night.
screenSummary.controlArea: The control area has a collapsible panel overlaid on the sky with location, latitude, longitude, date and time presets, year/month/day/hour jump controls, play/pause, field of view, display options, and a Reset All button. Preferences → Simulation controls star names, constellation lines, planet names, and a deeper star catalog.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Drag the sky to look around, click or press N or P to select a star or planet for details, use the wheel to zoom, and press play to watch the sky rotate as time advances.

a11y.currentDetails: Latitude {{latitude}}°, looking toward azimuth {{azimuth}}° at altitude {{altitude}}°, local sidereal time {{lst}} h. The simulation is {{playState}}.
a11y.selectedAnnouncement: {{name}}, magnitude {{mag}}, right ascension {{ra}} hours, declination {{dec}} degrees, altitude {{alt}} degrees, azimuth {{az}} degrees.
a11y.trackingOn: Tracking the selected object. The view now follows it as time passes.
a11y.trackingNoSelection: Select a star or planet first, then press T to track it.
a11y.measureFirstPoint: First measurement point set. Shift-click a second point to measure the angle between them.

controls.skyViewHelp: Arrow keys pan the view, and holding Control with the arrows advances civil time; Escape clears the selection. These shortcuts also work anywhere in the simulation: J, K, and L slow, reset, and speed up the time rate; plus and minus zoom in and out; Shift plus N, S, E, W, or Z looks toward north, south, east, west, or the zenith; N and P cycle through stars and planets in view; T tracks the selected object as time passes; and A, G, Q, Z, E, and M toggle the atmosphere, horizon, cardinal directions, alt-azimuth grid, equatorial grid, and meridian. The wheel changes field of view. Click a star or planet to select it.
controls.controlPanelHelpExpanded: Collapse to reduce overlay over the sky.
controls.controlPanelHelpCollapsed: Expand to show field of view, magnitude, and display options.
controls.timePanelHelpExpanded: Collapse to reduce overlay over the sky.
controls.timePanelHelpCollapsed: Expand to show date, time, and playback controls.
controls.now: Set date and time to now
controls.observerLocationPanelHelpExpanded: Collapse to reduce overlay over the sky.
controls.observerLocationPanelHelpCollapsed: Expand to show the location map and latitude / longitude controls.
controls.observerLocationMapHelp: Drag the pin on the world map to set your latitude and longitude.
controls.useMyLocationHelp: Set latitude and longitude from your device, asking permission if needed.
controls.useMyLocationError: Couldn't find your location. Set latitude and longitude manually or drag the map.
controls.showEquatorialGrid: Show equatorial RA and Dec grid
controls.showAtmosphere: Show atmospheric sky colors and daytime star fade
controls.trueScaleBodies: Size planet discs by their real angular size (exaggerated for visibility), so they grow and shrink with distance, and show the illuminated phase of Mercury, Venus, and Mars. The Sun and Moon are always true scale.
controls.showEcliptic: Show the ecliptic, the Sun's yearly path
controls.showObjectPath: Show the daily path of the selected object across the sky
controls.trackSelected: Track the selected object, keeping it centered as time passes
controls.measureToolHelp: Click two points or objects to measure the angle between them. Click empty sky to clear.
controls.infoButton: Info — how to use this simulation

keyboardHelp.lookPanDescription: Arrow keys pan azimuth and altitude. Drag with the mouse for the same motion.
keyboardHelp.lookNorthDescription: Shift plus N turns the view to face north, keeping the current altitude.
keyboardHelp.lookSouthDescription: Shift plus S turns the view to face south, keeping the current altitude.
keyboardHelp.lookEastDescription: Shift plus E turns the view to face east, keeping the current altitude.
keyboardHelp.lookWestDescription: Shift plus W turns the view to face west, keeping the current altitude.
keyboardHelp.lookZenithDescription: Shift plus Z points the view straight up at the zenith.
keyboardHelp.zoomDescription: Press plus to zoom in and minus to zoom out. Scroll the mouse wheel for the same effect.
keyboardHelp.advanceCivilTimeDescription: Ctrl plus left or right arrow advances or rewinds civil time. Matches Ctrl-drag.
keyboardHelp.timeSlowerDescription: J steps the playback rate one notch slower, crossing into reverse past normal speed.
keyboardHelp.timeNormalDescription: K restores the playback rate to normal forward speed.
keyboardHelp.timeFasterDescription: L steps the playback rate one notch faster.
keyboardHelp.timeDayForwardDescription: Shift plus plus advances the clock by one sidereal day so the stars return to place.
keyboardHelp.timeDayBackwardDescription: Shift plus minus rewinds the clock by one sidereal day.
keyboardHelp.selectNextDescription: N selects the next named star or planet in the current view.
keyboardHelp.selectPreviousDescription: P selects the previous named star or planet in the current view.
keyboardHelp.clearSelectionDescription: Escape clears the selected star or planet.
keyboardHelp.trackObjectDescription: T follows the selected star or planet, keeping it centered as time passes.
keyboardHelp.toggleAtmosphereDescription: A turns the atmospheric sky colors and daytime star fade on or off.
keyboardHelp.toggleHorizonDescription: G turns the ground band and horizon line on or off.
keyboardHelp.toggleCardinalsDescription: Q turns the north, south, east, and west labels on or off.
keyboardHelp.toggleGridDescription: Z turns the altitude-azimuth grid on or off.
keyboardHelp.toggleEquatorialGridDescription: E turns the equatorial right-ascension and declination grid on or off.
keyboardHelp.toggleMeridianDescription: M turns the local meridian line on or off.

preferences.title: Simulation
preferences.deepStarCatalog: Deeper star catalog (fainter stars to mag 7.5)

info.title: How to use
info.intro: This is a first-person view of the night sky. Set where and when you are observing, then look around and explore.
info.locationTip: Set your location. Use the Observer location panel to drag the map pin or type a latitude and longitude — or press "Use my location".
info.timeTip: Set the date and time. Use the Time panel to jump to "Now", step forward or back a sidereal day, or press play and change the rate to watch the sky turn.
info.lookTip: Look around. Drag the sky to pan your view, or use the arrow keys. Look toward a cardinal direction to reorient.
info.zoomTip: Zoom in and out. Scroll the mouse wheel or change the Field of view to see a wider or narrower patch of sky.
info.searchTip: Find an object. Type a star or planet name in the search box at the top, then press Enter to select and track it.
info.displayTip: Show more or less. Use the Display panel to toggle grids, cardinal directions, the meridian, and how faint a star can be before it is hidden.
